title: API Reference
content: API Reference  The GCXONE API allows developers to integrate third-party applications with the platform. It is a RESTful API that uses JSON for request and response bodies.

title: API Reference
content: All API requests should be made to:

title: API Reference
content: The API uses Bearer Token authentication. You must include your API Key in the Authorization header of each request.    Generating an API Key 1.  Log in to the Console as an Admin. 2.  Go to Settings > API Keys. 3.  Click Generate New Key.

title: API Reference
content: Devices  GET /devices Returns a list of all devices assigned to your organization.  Response:   GET /devices/{id} Returns details for a specific device.  Alarms  GET /alarms Returns a list of active alarms.  POST /alarms/{id}/acknowledge Acknowledges an alarm.  Request Body:

title: API Reference
content: The API is rate-limited to 100 requests per minute per IP address. Exceeding this limit will result in a 429 Too Many Requests response.

title: BulkImport
content: BulkImport  Importieren Sie viele Geräte mit wenigen Klicks.   BulkImport ermöglicht es Ihnen, eine Excel-Vorlagendatei hochzuladen, die Datensätze in der Vorschau anzuzeigen und Geräte in einem geführten Ablauf zu erstellen.  <video   controls   style={{     maxWidth: '100%',     borderRadius: '8px

title: BulkImport
content: Öffnen Sie GCXONE, gehen Sie zu Geräte und wählen Sie dann Importieren.  !Geräteseite mit Import-Schaltfläche  Das System öffnet einen dreistufigen Assistenten:  1. Hochladen   2. Vorschau   3. Abschluss    Sie bleiben die ganze Zeit auf der Geräteseite.

title: BulkImport
content: Verwenden Sie BulkImport, wenn Sie:  - Viele Geräte für einen Kunden oder Standort hinzufügen möchten.   - Geräte von einer älteren Plattform migrieren.   - Neue Hardware gleichzeitig an mehreren Standorten ausrollen.   - Die Gerätekonfiguration konsistent halten möchten, indem Sie mit einer Vorlage

title: BulkImport
content: !Upload-Schritt - Excel-Datei auswählen  Der Upload-Schritt bietet zwei Schaltflächen:  - Vorlagendatei herunterladen     Lädt eine leere Excel-Datei mit allen erforderlichen Spalten herunter.  - Dateien durchsuchen     Öffnet eine Dateiauswahl, in der Sie Ihre ausgefüllte .xlsx- oder .xls-Datei aus

title: BulkImport
sectionTitle: Schritt 2 - Importdaten in der Vorschau anzeigen
content: !Vorschau-Schritt - Zeilen überprüfen  Der Vorschau-Schritt zeigt:  - Den Kunden oder Standort oben.   - Eine Tabelle aller Zeilen, die BulkImport in der Datei erkannt hat.   - Die Gesamtzahl der Zeilen auf der rechten Seite.    Überprüfen Sie mindestens:  - Standortname.   - Gerätetyp und Modell.

title: BulkImport
content: !Abschluss-Schritt - Erfolg  Nachdem der Import abgeschlossen ist, sehen Sie einen Abschlussbildschirm.  Sie erhalten:  - Eine klare Erfolgsmeldung.   - Die Anzahl der Datensätze, die BulkImport importiert hat.    Wenn das System Probleme mit einigen Zeilen gefunden hat, zeigt es auch:  - Die Anzahl

title: BulkImport
content: Sie erhalten immer die neueste Vorlage, indem Sie im Upload-Schritt auf Vorlagendatei herunterladen klicken.  !Beispiel für Excel-Vorlage  Die Vorlage enthält diese Spalten:  | Spaltenname                  | Beschreibung

title: BulkImport
content: BulkImport validiert Daten während der Vorschau und während des eigentlichen Imports.  Vorschau-Prüfungen  Während der Vorschau prüft BulkImport auf:  - Fehlende Pflichtangaben.   - Ungültige Portnummern.   - Grundlegende Formatprobleme bei IP-Adressen oder Hostnamen.   - Offensichtliche Duplikate i

title: BulkImport
content: - Beginnen Sie mit einer kleinen Testdatei, zum Beispiel mit fünf Geräten, um das Format zu bestätigen.   - Verwenden Sie eine Datei pro Kunde oder Region, damit die Vorschau lesbar bleibt.   - Bewahren Sie eine Kopie jeder importierten Datei auf, falls Sie später Änderungen nachvollziehen müssen.

title: BulkImport
content: Kann ich BulkImport mit derselben Datei erneut ausführen?   Ja. Dies kann Duplikate erstellen, wenn Ihre Umgebung diese nicht blockiert. Überprüfen Sie daher die Vorschau sorgfältig.   Im Zweifelsfall bewahren Sie eine Kopie der Originaldatei auf und verwenden Sie kleinere Dateien für Wiederholungsv

title: BulkImport
content: BulkImport  Import many devices in a few clicks.   BulkImport lets you upload a template Excel file, preview the records, and create devices in one guided flow.  <video   controls   style={{     maxWidth: '100%',     borderRadius: '8px',     boxShadow: '0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.25)',     m

title: BulkImport
content: Open GCXONE, go to Devices, then select Import.  !Devices page with Import button  The system opens a three step wizard:  1. Upload   2. Preview   3. Complete    You stay on the Devices page the whole time.

title: BulkImport
content: Use BulkImport when you want to:  - Add many devices for a customer or site.   - Move devices from an older platform.   - Roll out new hardware to several locations at once.   - Keep device configuration consistent by starting from one template file.    For a single device, use the normal dev

title: BulkImport
content: !Upload step - select Excel file  The Upload step gives two buttons:  - Download Template File     Downloads an empty Excel file with all required columns.  - Browse Files     Opens a file picker where you choose your filled .xlsx or .xls file.  The wizard accepts one Excel file per run.

title: BulkImport
content: !Preview step - review rows  The Preview step shows:  - The customer or site at the top.   - A table of all rows that BulkImport detected in the file.   - The total number of rows on the right.    Check at least:  - Site name.   - Device type and model.   - Serial numbers and IP addresse

title: BulkImport
content: !Complete step - success  After the import finishes you see a completion screen.  You get:  - A clear success message.   - The number of records that BulkImport imported.    If the system found issues with some rows, it also shows:  - The count of failed records.   - A link to download a

title: BulkImport
content: You always get the latest template by clicking Download Template File in the Upload step.  !Template Excel example  The template contains these columns:  | Column name                  | Description                                                                   | | ------------------------

title: BulkImport
content: BulkImport validates data during preview and during the actual import.  Preview checks  During preview BulkImport checks for:  - Missing required values.   - Invalid port numbers.   - Basic IP or hostname format problems.   - Obvious duplicates in the same file.    If BulkImport finds cri

title: BulkImport
content: - Start with a small test file, for example five devices, to confirm the format.   - Use one file per customer or region so previews stay readable.   - Keep a copy of every file you import in case you need to audit changes later.   - After a large import, open a few devices in the UI and confirm

title: BulkImport
content: Can I rerun BulkImport with the same file?   Yes. This can create duplicates if your environment does not block them, so check the preview carefully.   When in doubt, keep a copy of the original file and use smaller files for retries.  What happens to rows that fail?   Failed rows do not create

content: TowerGuard  Deploy Towers 3x Faster. With Zero Site Downtime.  Revolutionary tower deployment system that dramatically accelerates security infrastructure installation while maintaining continuous site protection throughout the deployment process.

title: content

content: TowerGuard begins with a site assessment to determine optimal temporary and permanent placement strategies. Once approved, temporary units deploy immediately while permanent installation proceeds in parallel, ensuring your site never loses protection during the transition process.

title: content
content: ZenMode  Less Distraction. More Action.  A focused, distraction-free interface designed for security operators who need to concentrate on critical tasks without unnecessary visual clutter or interruptions.

title: content
content: Zen Mode transforms your security monitoring experience by removing non-essential interface elements, reducing visual noise, and creating a clean, focused environment that enhances operator concentration and decision-making.

title: content
content: - Minimalist Interface: Clean, uncluttered workspace design - Priority Filtering: Show only high-priority alerts and events - Distraction Blocking: Hide non-critical notifications and widgets - Customizable Layout: Tailor the interface to your workflow - One-Click Toggle: Instantly switch betwee

title: Account Setup
content: Account Setup  Follow these steps to set up your GCXONE account.

title: Account Setup
content: 1.  Navigate to the GCXONE login page. 2.  Click Register. 3.  Fill in your organization details and administrator contact information. 4.  Verify your email address.

title: Account Setup
content: Once logged in, you will be guided through the initial setup wizard to: - Add your first site. - Connect devices. - Invite team members.

title: Getting Started
content: Getting Started with GCXONE  Welcome to the GCXONE documentation. GCXONE is a fully managed alarm management platform designed to automate repetitive tasks, ensure reliable alarm transmission, and provide seamless integration with IoT devices.

title: Getting Started
content: GCXONE acts as the central nervous system for your security and building automation infrastructure. It connects devices, processes alarms using intelligent workflows, and notifies the right people at the right time.  Key Features     Universal Connectivity: Connect any device via standard protoc

title: Getting Started
content: Before you begin, ensure you have the following:  1.  GCXONE Account: You should have received an invitation email to activate your account. 2.  Supported Browser: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge (latest versions). 3.  Network Access: Ensure your firewall allows outbound connections to .gcxone.

title: Getting Started
content: 1. Log in to the Console Navigate to https://console.gcxone.com and log in with your credentials.  2. Configure your Profile Go to Settings > Profile to set your time zone and notification preferences.  3. Connect your First Device Follow the Installer Guide to provision your first gateway or

content: For Admins: Configure Users and Permissions    For Operators: Learn how to Process Alarms    For Developers: Explore the API

title: Admin Guide
content: Admin Guide  As a System Administrator, you have full control over the GCXONE organization. This guide covers the essential tasks for managing your environment.

title: Admin Guide
content: Inviting Users 1.  Navigate to Settings > Users. 2.  Click Invite User. 3.  Enter the email address and select a Role (Admin, Operator, Installer). 4.  Click Send Invitation.  Managing Roles & Permissions GCXONE uses a Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) model:  | Role          | Description

title: Admin Guide
content: Creating a Site A "Site" represents a physical location (e.g., "Main Office", "Warehouse A"). 1.  Go to Sites > Create New. 2.  Enter the Site Name and Address. 3.  Assign a Time Zone.  Global Settings    Security Policies: Enforce 2FA for all users under Settings > Security.    Audit Logs:

title: Admin Guide
content: Manage your subscription plan and view invoices in the Billing portal. You can upgrade your plan to add more devices or retention storage at any time.

title: Installer Guide
content: Installer Guide  This guide is for Installers and Technicians responsible for setting up the physical hardware and connecting it to the GCXONE platform.

title: Installer Guide
content: Step 1: Unbox and Power On Connect the GCXONE Gateway to power and Ethernet. Ensure the Power and Network LEDs are solid green.  Step 2: Register the Device 1.  Log in to the Installer App on your mobile device. 2.  Scan the QR code on the bottom of the gateway. 3.  Assign the device to a Site

title: Installer Guide
content: Adding Sensors 1.  Put the gateway into Pairing Mode via the app. 2.  Trigger the sensor (e.g., open the door contact). 3.  Name the sensor (e.g., "Front Door") and assign a Zone Type.  Testing Perform a Walk Test to verify all sensors are communicating correctly.    Go to Diagnostics > Walk

title: Installer Guide
content: | Issue                  | Possible Cause         | Solution                                                 | | :--------------------- | :--------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------- | | Gateway Offline    | No Internet connection | Check Ethernet cable and fir

title: Operator Guide
content: Operator Guide  As an Operator, your primary responsibility is to monitor the system for alarms and take appropriate action. The GCXONE Dashboard is your command center.

title: Operator Guide
content: The Dashboard provides a real-time overview of all connected sites and devices.    Active Alarms: High-priority events requiring immediate attention.    Device Status: Health check of all gateways and sensors.    Recent Activity: A live feed of system events.

title: Operator Guide
content: When an alarm is triggered (e.g., "Motion Detected" or "Door Forced Open"):  1.  Notification: You will hear an audible alert and see a pop-up on the dashboard. 2.  Acknowledge: Click the alarm to Acknowledge it. This lets other operators know you are handling it. 3.  Investigate: Review the eve

title: Operator Guide
content: You can generate reports to analyze system activity over time. 1.  Navigate to the Reports tab. 2.  Select a Report Type (e.g., "Alarm Summary", "User Access"). 3.  Choose a Date Range. 4.  Click Generate PDF or Export CSV.
